description: A macOS-focused Tauri desktop command center for managing multiple projects and AI coding agents concurrently while keeping the user focused on one active thing at a time. The app coordinates tasks, agent worktrees, embedded terminals, PR/self-review, GitHub status, plugins, and contextual nudges so it interrupts the user only when attention is useful.

goals.description: Manage multiple software projects and multiple AI agents running at the same time from a single desktop app.
Help the user focus by presenting one actionable item at a time and reducing distracting context switching.
Poke or notify the user at the right moments, such as when an agent needs input, a review is ready, CI changes state, or a task is blocked, and otherwise stay out of the way.
Run AI agents in isolated git worktrees with tracked task lifecycle, terminals, CI status, and review loops.
Support extension through built-in and managed plugins while preserving predictable host/runtime behavior.

languages.name: TypeScript
languages.role: frontend, plugin SDK, built-in plugins, tests

languages.name: Rust
languages.role: Tauri backend, database, commands, GitHub, PTY, providers

languages.name: CSS
languages.role: Tailwind CSS v4 CSS-first configuration and component scoped styles

architecture.pattern: Desktop application monorepo with a Svelte 5/TypeScript frontend, Rust/Tauri v2 backend, SQLite persistence, managed plugin workspace, and AI-provider integrations.

modules.name: Frontend app
modules.description: Svelte UI for board/task management, settings, PR/self-review diffs, terminals, skills, and typed IPC-facing business logic.

modules.name: Reusable components
modules.description: Dialogs, file viewers, task detail surfaces, context menus, and view components.

modules.name: Frontend library
modules.description: Business logic utilities, stores/composables, typed IPC wrappers, terminalPool lifecycle owner, diff/file helpers, and settings helpers.

modules.name: Rust commands
modules.description: Tauri command boundary for tasks, projects, config, providers, GitHub, review flows, plugins, PTY, and orchestration.

modules.name: Database layer
modules.description: SQLite migrations and domain-specific Database impl blocks for app persistence.

modules.name: Provider/runtime integration
modules.description: Claude Code, OpenCode, and Pi agent provider integration.

modules.name: Plugin platform
modules.description: Workspace SDK plus built-in plugin implementations.

architecture.dataFlow: Svelte components call typed wrappers in src/lib/ipc.ts. Those wrappers invoke Tauri commands in Rust, which coordinate SQLite domain modules, Git/GitHub clients, PTY managers, agent providers, plugin host/RPC, and local HTTP/MCP services. Agent tasks are launched in isolated git worktrees and reflected back through task lifecycle, terminal output, CI status, and review UI.

workflows.name: development
workflows.description: Run and iterate on the app locally.

workflows.name: agent-task-flow
workflows.description: Create a task, launch an AI provider, let it work in an isolated worktree, then review changes and PR status.

processes.name: Project Install
processes.description: Project onboarding/profile generation process used to configure babysitter for this repository.

processes.name: TDD Quality Convergence
processes.description: Recommended default methodology for feature and bugfix tasks: write/update tests first, implement, verify, refine.

services.name: OpenCode
services.type: AI coding agent service via HTTP/SSE

painPoints.description: Async frontend/component cleanup and listener registration have required stabilization work.
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Keep test cleanup explicit and add regression tests for listener lifecycle changes.

painPoints.description: Terminal/PTY lifecycle, shell tabs, stale event filtering, and shortcut ownership are high-risk areas.
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Preserve terminalPool as single lifecycle owner and require focused tests for any PTY/session changes.

painPoints.description: Plugin runtime packaging and built-in plugin builds are sensitive to CI and host/runtime path assumptions.
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Verify plugin changes with build:plugins plus host/runtime tests.

painPoints.description: The product must balance visibility across many agents/projects with a calm one-thing-at-a-time focus model.
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Favor attention queues, contextual nudges, and explicit focus modes over broad dashboards that demand constant scanning.

bottlenecks.description: Top-level app routing/state orchestration is high-churn.
bottlenecks.impact: Navigation and global state changes can cause conflicts or broad tests.

bottlenecks.description: Rust main/bootstrap command registration is high-churn.
bottlenecks.impact: Backend additions can conflict around setup and command registration.

bottlenecks.description: Frontend shared types and IPC wrappers are tightly coupled to Rust command payloads.
bottlenecks.impact: Payload naming mismatches can break functionality silently.

bottlenecks.description: GitHub polling/comment ingestion combines async external API data with local persistence.
bottlenecks.impact: Risk of duplicate/stale comments or status races.

bottlenecks.description: Terminal lifecycle is regression-prone and must remain single-owned by terminalPool.
bottlenecks.impact: Incorrect liveness/session state can lose output or misrepresent running agents.

naming.frontend: PascalCase Svelte components; camelCase/lowercase TS utilities; tests colocated as *.test.ts or *.svelte.test.ts.
naming.rust: snake_case modules/functions; domain DB modules under src-tauri/src/db.
naming.tasks: T-<number> references are app-local task IDs.

git.commitStyle: conventional-ish prefixes plus KVG task references in branches/PRs

codeStyle.svelte: Svelte 5 runes only: $state, $derived, $effect, $props() with local Props interfaces; on-prefixed callbacks; no legacy event dispatcher.
codeStyle.typescript: Use import type under verbatimModuleSyntax; nullable fields use T | null rather than optional.
codeStyle.styling: Tailwind utilities and daisyUI semantic classes; Tailwind v4 CSS-first config in src/app.css; no hardcoded hex colors.
codeStyle.rust: Tauri commands return Result<T, String> with contextual map_err(format!(...)); DB domain files use impl super::Database.

conventions.errorHandling: Rust command boundaries map errors to Result<T, String>; frontend uses typed IPC wrappers and UI state; avoid direct invoke() usage outside src/lib/ipc.ts.
conventions.testingConventions: Use TDD. Write/update tests first, verify failure where practical, then implement. Cover business logic and behavior; do not assert Tailwind/CSS visual classes. Run pnpm test and/or cargo test depending on touched area.
additionalRules: All Tauri invoke calls go through src/lib/ipc.ts wrappers.
External links use openUrl() IPC wrapper.
Map-based stores require new Map() assignment for reactivity.
Task context menus use TaskContextMenu; non-task menus use ContextMenu primitives.
Plain-key vim bindings must use useVimNavigation and check isInputFocused().
Multi-shell PTY sessions are keyed per shell tab end-to-end; terminal lifecycle ownership belongs in src/lib/terminalPool.ts.
Do not use $effect return cleanup to release resources keyed by prop value; compare previous logical value and use onDestroy for teardown.

claudeMdInstructions: Open Forge product goal: coordinate multiple projects and AI agents while preserving one-thing-at-a-time focus and low-noise timely nudges.
Use /babysitter:project-install to refresh project profile artifacts after major architecture, workflow, or product-direction changes.
Prefer TDD-driven iterative convergence for implementation tasks and run the relevant pnpm/cargo verification gates.
Map code before broad changes touching high-churn integration points: App.svelte, main.rs, ipc/types, github_poller, and terminalPool.
CI/CD babysitter integration was intentionally skipped; do not add GitHub Actions babysitter automation without a future explicit task.

preferences.attentionModel: Interrupt only for meaningful decisions, blocked agents, review readiness, CI failures, destructive operations, or workflow handoffs.
